title: GPT-6 Is Launching Into a World OpenAI No Longer Controls
summary: The video argues that GPT-6's delayed release has allowed Microsoft, Apple, and Google to move away from OpenAI's models, with Microsoft integrating Project Polaris into GitHub Copilot, Apple adopting Gemini, and Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 reportedly achieving higher benchmark scores than GPT-5.5. The piece frames this as a loss of OpenAI's market dominance amid broader industry shifts.
writeup: The video argues that delays to GPT-6 have given OpenAI's largest partners and rivals room to shift away from its models. It cites Microsoft integrating Project Polaris into GitHub Copilot, Apple adopting Gemini, and Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 reportedly scoring higher on benchmarks than GPT-5.5. The framing is a loss of OpenAI's market dominance, with the timing of a release mattering as much as the model itself when competitors capture key API integrations.
